21.iana_status: Defined TXT keys: u=<username> p=<password> path=<path>
pentest_notes: Brute force: hydra -l admin -P wordlist.txt ftp://<host>
defensive_notes: Replace FTP with SFTP (port 22) or FTPS
Restrict to known IPs via firewall

top_cves.description: A race condition in the authentication agent mechanism of sshd 1.2.17 allows an attacker to steal another user's credentials.
An SSH server allows authentication through the .rhosts file.
OpenSSH does not properly drop privileges when the UseLogin option is enabled, which allows local users to execute arbitrary commands by providing the command to the ssh daemon.
sshd program in the Rapidstream 2.1 Beta VPN appliance has a hard-coded "rsadmin" account with a null password, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via ssh.
Format string vulnerabilities in OpenBSD ssh program (and possibly other BSD-based operating systems) allow attackers to gain root privileges.

pentest_notes: Check PasswordAuthentication and PermitRootLogin in sshd_config
Brute force: hydra -l root -P wordlist.txt ssh://<host>
defensive_notes: Disable PasswordAuthentication — enforce key-based auth only
Bind to non-default port if possible (security-through-obscurity bonus)

top_cves.description: Telnet allows a remote client to specify environment variables including LD_LIBRARY_PATH, allowing an attacker to bypass the normal system libraries and gain root access.
Buffer overflow in telnet daemon tgetent routing allows remote attackers to gain root access via the TERMCAP environmental variable.
A memory leak in a Motorola CableRouter allows remote attackers to conduct a denial of service via a large number of telnet connections.
Denial of service in telnet from the Windows NT Resource Kit, by opening then immediately closing a connection.
A router's configuration service or management interface (such as a web server or telnet) is configured to allow connections from arbitrary hosts.

pentest_notes: Telnet sends credentials in plaintext — sniff with Wireshark
Brute force: hydra -l admin -P wordlist.txt telnet://<host>
Check open relay: RCPT TO external address
defensive_notes: Restrict to known sending IPs (no open relay)

pentest_notes: DNS version: dig version.bind chaos txt @<host>
Tools: dnsx, dnsrecon, fierce, nmap --script dns*
defensive_notes: Disable zone transfers to unauthorised IPs

80.iana_status: Defined TXT keys: u=<username> p=<password> path=<path to document>
pentest_notes: Directory brute: gobuster dir -u http://<host> -w wordlist.txt
Tools: burpsuite, nikto, gobuster, ffuf, nuclei

pentest_notes: Banner grab: nc -nv <host> 110 → USER test PASS test

techniques.name: Data from Configuration Repository: SNMP MIB Dump

top_cves.description: Buffer overflow in (1) HttpSaveCVP.dll and (2) HttpSaveCSP.dll in Trend Micro InterScan VirusWall 3.5.1 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.

Multiple unspecified vulnerabilities in Opera 8.50 on Linux and Windows have unknown impact and attack vectors, related to (1) " handling of must-revalidate cache directive for HTTPS pages" or (2) a "display issue with cookie comment encoding."
Unspecified vulnerability in Oracle HTTP Server 9.2.0.7 and Oracle Collaboration Suite 9.0.4.2 has unknown impact and remote attack vectors related to HTTPS and SSL, aka Vuln# OHS04.

Unspecified vulnerability in Oracle HTTP Server 9.2.0.7, Oracle Collaboration Suite 9.0.4.2, and Oracle E-Business Suite and Applications 11.5.10CU2 has unknown impact and remote attack vectors related to HTTPS and SSL, aka Vuln# OHS05.
Unspecified vulnerability in Oracle HTTP Server 9.2.0.7, when running on HP Tru64 UNIX, has unknown impact and remote attack vectors related to HTTPS and SSL, aka Vuln# OHS07.

pentest_notes: Check cipher suites, certificate expiry, HSTS
defensive_notes: Rotate and monitor certificates (use ACME/Let's Encrypt)
Block at perimeter — SMB should NEVER be internet-facing

top_cves.description: In amcs_cdev_unlocked_ioctl of audiometrics.c, there is a possible out of bounds write due to improper input validation. This could lead to local escalation of privilege with System execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation.Product: AndroidVersions: Android kernelAndroid ID: A-206128522References: N/A

An issue was discovered in AMCS Group Trux Waste Management Software before version 7.19.0018.26912, allows local attackers to obtain sensitive information via a static, hard-coded AES Key-IV pair in the TxUtilities.dll and TruxUser.cfg components.
